content.json:
## Load Balancer Configuration Dashboard

This workbook provides a comprehensive view of all Load Balancers in your Azure subscription, including their configuration and related components.

parameters.name: ResourceGroup
parameters.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| summarize by resourceGroup
| order by resourceGroup asc
| project value = resourceGroup, label = resourceGroup, selected = false

parameters.name: SelectedRule
parameters.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand rule = properties.loadBalancingRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(rule.name)
| project value = RuleName, label = RuleName, selected = false
| order by label asc

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where resourceGroup in ({ResourceGroup})
| extend SKU = properties.sku.name
| extend Tier = properties.sku.tier
| extend ProvisioningState = properties.provisioningState
| extend FrontendIPCount = array_length(properties.frontendIPConfigurations)
| extend BackendPools = array_length(properties.backendAddressPools)
| extend LoadBalancingRules = array_length(properties.loadBalancingRules)
| extend HealthProbes = array_length(properties.probes)
| extend InboundNATRules = array_length(properties.inboundNatRules)
| extend OutboundRules = array_length(properties.outboundRules)
| project Name = name, ResourceGroup = resourceGroup, Location = location, SKU, Tier, ProvisioningState, ['Frontend IPs'] = FrontendIPCount, ['Backend Pools'] = BackendPools, ['LB Rules'] = LoadBalancingRules, ['Health Probes'] = HealthProbes, ['NAT Rules'] = InboundNATRules, ['Outbound Rules'] = OutboundRules, id
content.title: Load Balancers
content.noDataMessage: No Load Balancers found in the selected subscription(s) and resource group(s)

content.json:
---
## Rules & Backend Configuration Status

**Note:** Status below shows configuration state only. For actual backend health (probe results), see the "Health Probe Status by Backend" chart in the Metrics tab after selecting a Load Balancer.

content.query:
resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| extend LBName = split(id, '/')[-1]
| mv-expand rule = properties.loadBalancingRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(rule.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(rule.properties.protocol)
| extend FrontendPort = tostring(rule.properties.frontendPort)
| extend BackendPort = tostring(rule.properties.backendPort)
| extend BackendPoolNameFromRule = tostring(split(rule.properties.backendAddressPool.id, '/')[-1])
| extend ProbeName = tostring(split(rule.properties.probe.id, '/')[-1])
| extend RuleProvisioningState = tostring(rule.properties.provisioningState)
| mv-expand backendPool = properties.backendAddressPools
| extend CurrentPoolName = tostring(backendPool.name)
| where CurrentPoolName == BackendPoolNameFromRule
| extend BackendPoolProvisioningState = tostring(backendPool.properties.provisioningState)
| extend BackendCount = array_length(backendPool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses)
| extend HealthStatus = case(
    RuleProvisioningState != "Succeeded", "Misconfigured",
    BackendPoolProvisioningState != "Succeeded", "Misconfigured",
    BackendCount == 0, "No Backends",
    "Configured"
  )
| project ['Load Balancer'] = LBName, ['Rule'] = RuleName, Protocol, ['Frontend Port'] = FrontendPort, ['Backend Port'] = BackendPort, ['Backend Pool'] = BackendPoolNameFromRule, ['Backends Count'] = BackendCount, ['Health Probe'] = ProbeName, ['Status'] = HealthStatus, ['Provisioning State'] = RuleProvisioningState
content.title: Load Balancing Rules with Backend Health Overview

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| mv-expand backendPool = properties.backendAddressPools
| extend LBName = split(id, '/')[-1]
| extend PoolName = tostring(backendPool.name)
| extend LoadBalancerBackendAddresses = backendPool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses
| mv-expand address = LoadBalancerBackendAddresses
| extend IPAddress = tostring(address.properties.ipAddress)
| extend NICId = tostring(address.properties.networkInterfaceIPConfiguration.id)
| extend VMName = coalesce(split(NICId, '/')[-3], 'External IP')
| extend VMNICName = split(NICId, '/')[-1]
| extend BackendStatus = 'Configured'
| project ['Load Balancer'] = LBName, ['Backend Pool'] = PoolName, ['Backend Target'] = VMName, ['VM NIC'] = VMNICName, ['IP Address'] = IPAddress, ['Status'] = BackendStatus
content.title: Backend Pool Members (Configured Targets)

content.json:
---
### Detailed Configuration

**Selected Load Balancer:** {SelectedLoadBalancerName}

Select a Load Balancer from the table above to view detailed configuration.

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| project Configuration = 'General', ['SKU Name'] = properties.sku.name, ['SKU Tier'] = properties.sku.tier, ['Provisioning State'] = properties.provisioningState
| union (Resources
  | where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
  | where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
  | project Configuration = 'Counts', ['Frontend IPs'] = array_length(properties.frontendIPConfigurations), ['Backend Pools'] = array_length(properties.backendAddressPools), ['Load Balancing Rules'] = array_length(properties.loadBalancingRules), ['Health Probes'] = array_length(properties.probes))
| union (Resources
  | where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
  | where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
  | project Configuration = 'NAT', ['Inbound NAT Rules'] = array_length(properties.inboundNatRules), ['Inbound NAT Pools'] = array_length(properties.inboundNatPools), ['Outbound Rules'] = array_length(properties.outboundRules))
content.title: Basic Configuration

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand frontendIP = properties.frontendIPConfigurations
| extend FrontendName = tostring(frontendIP.name)
| extend PrivateIP = tostring(frontendIP.properties.privateIPAddress)
| extend PrivateIPAllocation = tostring(frontendIP.properties.privateIPAllocationMethod)
| extend PublicIPId = tostring(frontendIP.properties.publicIPAddress.id)
| extend PublicIPName = split(PublicIPId, '/')[-1]
| extend SubnetId = tostring(frontendIP.properties.subnet.id)
| extend SubnetName = split(SubnetId, '/')[-1]
| extend Zone = tostring(frontendIP.zones)
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(frontendIP.properties.provisioningState)
| project ['Frontend IP'] = FrontendName, ['Private IP'] = PrivateIP, ['Private IP Allocation'] = PrivateIPAllocation, ['Public IP'] = PublicIPName, ['Subnet'] = SubnetName, ['Zone'] = Zone, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Frontend IP Configurations

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand backendPool = properties.backendAddressPools
| extend PoolName = tostring(backendPool.name)
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(backendPool.properties.provisioningState)
| extend LoadBalancerBackendAddresses = backendPool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses
| extend BackendIPConfigs = backendPool.properties.backendIPConfigurations
| extend AddressCount = coalesce(array_length(LoadBalancerBackendAddresses), 0)
| extend IPConfigCount = coalesce(array_length(BackendIPConfigs), 0)
| mv-expand address = LoadBalancerBackendAddresses
| extend IPAddress = tostring(address.properties.ipAddress)
| extend VMName = tostring(split(address.properties.networkInterfaceIPConfiguration.id, '/')[-3])
| extend Target = coalesce(IPAddress, VMName, '')
| summarize Targets = make_list(Target) by PoolName, AddressCount, IPConfigCount, ProvisioningState
| extend TargetsDisplay = iff(array_length(Targets) > 0 and Targets[0] != '', strcat_array(Targets, ', '), 'No targets configured')
| project ['Backend Pool'] = PoolName, ['Address Count'] = AddressCount, ['IP Config Count'] = IPConfigCount, ['Total Targets'] = AddressCount + IPConfigCount, ['Targets'] = TargetsDisplay, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Backend Address Pools

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand probe = properties.probes
| extend ProbeName = tostring(probe.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(probe.properties.protocol)
| extend Port = tostring(probe.properties.port)
| extend IntervalInSeconds = tostring(probe.properties.intervalInSeconds)
| extend NumberOfProbes = tostring(probe.properties.numberOfProbes)
| extend RequestPath = tostring(probe.properties.requestPath)
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(probe.properties.provisioningState)
| project ['Health Probe'] = ProbeName, Protocol, Port, ['Request Path'] = RequestPath, ['Interval (s)'] = IntervalInSeconds, ['Number of Probes'] = NumberOfProbes, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Health Probes

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand rule = properties.loadBalancingRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(rule.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(rule.properties.protocol)
| extend FrontendPort = tostring(rule.properties.frontendPort)
| extend BackendPort = tostring(rule.properties.backendPort)
| extend EnableFloatingIP = tostring(rule.properties.enableFloatingIP)
| extend EnableTcpReset = tostring(rule.properties.enableTcpReset)
| extend IdleTimeoutInMinutes = tostring(rule.properties.idleTimeoutInMinutes)
| extend LoadDistribution = tostring(rule.properties.loadDistribution)
| extend FrontendIPConfig = split(rule.properties.frontendIPConfiguration.id, '/')[-1]
| extend BackendPool = split(rule.properties.backendAddressPool.id, '/')[-1]
| extend Probe = split(rule.properties.probe.id, '/')[-1]
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(rule.properties.provisioningState)
| project ['Rule'] = RuleName, Protocol, ['Frontend Port'] = FrontendPort, ['Backend Port'] = BackendPort, ['Frontend IP'] = FrontendIPConfig, ['Backend Pool'] = BackendPool, ['Health Probe'] = Probe, ['Load Distribution'] = LoadDistribution, ['Floating IP'] = EnableFloatingIP, ['TCP Reset'] = EnableTcpReset, ['Idle Timeout (min)'] = IdleTimeoutInMinutes, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Load Balancing Rules

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand natRule = properties.inboundNatRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(natRule.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(natRule.properties.protocol)
| extend FrontendPort = tostring(natRule.properties.frontendPort)
| extend BackendPort = tostring(natRule.properties.backendPort)
| extend EnableFloatingIP = tostring(natRule.properties.enableFloatingIP)
| extend EnableTcpReset = tostring(natRule.properties.enableTcpReset)
| extend IdleTimeoutInMinutes = tostring(natRule.properties.idleTimeoutInMinutes)
| extend FrontendIPConfig = split(natRule.properties.frontendIPConfiguration.id, '/')[-1]
| extend BackendIPConfig = split(natRule.properties.backendIPConfiguration.id, '/')[-3]
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(natRule.properties.provisioningState)
| project ['NAT Rule'] = RuleName, Protocol, ['Frontend Port'] = FrontendPort, ['Backend Port'] = BackendPort, ['Frontend IP'] = FrontendIPConfig, ['Target VM'] = BackendIPConfig, ['Floating IP'] = EnableFloatingIP, ['TCP Reset'] = EnableTcpReset, ['Idle Timeout (min)'] = IdleTimeoutInMinutes, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Inbound NAT Rules

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand outboundRule = properties.outboundRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(outboundRule.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(outboundRule.properties.protocol)
| extend AllocatedOutboundPorts = tostring(outboundRule.properties.allocatedOutboundPorts)
| extend IdleTimeoutInMinutes = tostring(outboundRule.properties.idleTimeoutInMinutes)
| extend EnableTcpReset = tostring(outboundRule.properties.enableTcpReset)
| extend FrontendIPConfigs = array_length(outboundRule.properties.frontendIPConfigurations)
| extend BackendPool = split(outboundRule.properties.backendAddressPool.id, '/')[-1]
| extend ProvisioningState = tostring(outboundRule.properties.provisioningState)
| project ['Outbound Rule'] = RuleName, Protocol, ['Allocated Outbound Ports'] = AllocatedOutboundPorts, ['Frontend IP Configs'] = FrontendIPConfigs, ['Backend Pool'] = BackendPool, ['TCP Reset'] = EnableTcpReset, ['Idle Timeout (min)'] = IdleTimeoutInMinutes, ['Provisioning State'] = ProvisioningState
content.title: Outbound Rules

content.json:
---
### Rules Health Dashboard

**Instructions:** Click on a Load Balancing Rule below to view real-time health status of all backend components behind that rule.

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand rule = properties.loadBalancingRules
| extend RuleName = tostring(rule.name)
| extend Protocol = tostring(rule.properties.protocol)
| extend FrontendPort = tostring(rule.properties.frontendPort)
| extend BackendPort = tostring(rule.properties.backendPort)
| extend BackendPoolNameFromRule = tostring(split(rule.properties.backendAddressPool.id, '/')[-1])
| extend ProbeName = tostring(split(rule.properties.probe.id, '/')[-1])
| extend LBName = split(id, '/')[-1]
| extend LBResourceGroup = resourceGroup
| extend LBSubscription = subscriptionId
| project ['Rule Name'] = RuleName, Protocol, ['FE Port'] = FrontendPort, ['BE Port'] = BackendPort, ['Backend Pool'] = BackendPoolNameFromRule, ['Health Probe'] = ProbeName, RuleName, LBName, LBResourceGroup, LBSubscription
content.title: Load Balancing Rules
content.noDataMessage: Select a Load Balancer above to view its rules.

content.json:
---
### Backend Health Components
Real-time health status of all backend components behind **{SelectedRuleForHealth}**

content.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/loadbalancers"
| where id == "{SelectedLoadBalancer}"
| mv-expand rule = properties.loadBalancingRules
| where rule.name == "{SelectedRuleForHealth}"
| extend BackendPoolNameFromRule = tostring(split(rule.properties.backendAddressPool.id, '/')[-1])
| mv-expand backendPool = properties.backendAddressPools
| extend CurrentPoolName = tostring(backendPool.name)
| where CurrentPoolName == BackendPoolNameFromRule
| mv-expand address = backendPool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses
| extend IPAddress = tostring(address.properties.ipAddress)
| extend NICId = tostring(address.properties.networkInterfaceIPConfiguration.id)
| extend VMName = coalesce(split(NICId, '/')[-3], 'External IP')
| project ['IP Address'] = IPAddress, ['VM/Target'] = VMName, ['Status'] = 'Querying API...', ['Reason'] = 'Retrieving health...'
content.title: Backend Health Status
content.noDataMessage: Select a rule above to view backend health.
